name: Senolytics coverage repair assessment
summary: The senolytics track has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and pilot human studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. D+Q bone safety extraction now includes registry aggregates, full-text publication-table preferred-term rows beyond headache, and registry/article discontinuation fields. DKD, IPF, and AD-risk records now cite PubMed EFetch source snapshots for metadata-only abstract-level sample, dosing, timepoint, effect, and safety details, with the legacy IPF tolerability result reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot; those snapshots are not full-text/table sources. Registry surveillance now includes retained ClinicalTrials.gov v2 snapshots for deferred fisetin frailty registries NCT03430037 and NCT03675724, both active/enrolling-by-invitation with no posted results, but broader term screening found additional current senolytic/fisetin/D+Q candidates that remain deferred until their own snapshots are retained. The review landscape remains incomplete because no systematic-review or meta-analysis source was screened. Full article/table safety and efficacy extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplement material remains incomplete.

evidence_categories.rationale: Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF pilot, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk now have metadata-only PubMed EFetch snapshots supporting abstract-located extraction for sample, dosing, timing, or effect details.
Includes foundational discovery work plus the old-mouse healthspan and post-treatment survival paper.
Cellular senescence targeting is anchored by the discovery source and translation review, but pathway-level extraction is not yet complete.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future extraction should separate senescent-cell survival pathways, SASP markers, and tissue-specific clearance evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The IPF, bone, and AD-risk records explicitly encode mixed, pilot, primary/subgroup, and feasibility limitations instead of flattening them into positive claims; the AD-risk record now includes the abstract-level MoCA and TNF-alpha estimates.

Safety-oriented records now include the D+Q bone registry aggregate counts, full-text adverse-event table extraction, structured preferred-term rows for headache, diarrhea, nausea, constipation, gastrointestinal reflux, vertigo, fatigue, and body pain, and registry/article discontinuation fields. IPF and AD-risk safety slices now cite PubMed EFetch source snapshots for abstract-level safety statements, and DKD now has a metadata-only source-snapshot-backed abstract limitation record. Cross-human safety remains thin because DKD, IPF, and AD-risk still need full article/table safety extraction and harmonized event definitions.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details, reconcile D+Q bone supplement safety material if available, and keep cross-human safety synthesis blocked until term definitions, denominators, and relatedness/seriousness fields are harmonized.

evidence_categories.rationale: Four active no-results trial-watch records are now present, including source-snapshot-backed fisetin frailty registry records for NCT03430037 and NCT03675724. A bounded ClinicalTrials.gov term surveillance pass also logged additional direct active/recruiting senolytic hits that still need source snapshots before no-results records can be proposed.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Follow-up source snapshots are still needed for additional active/recruiting hits identified in the 2026-06-30 screening run, including direct D+Q and fisetin senolytic registries outside the two declared snapshot paths.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current slice includes a narrative translation review but no systematic-review or meta-analysis record.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Search specifically for recent systematic reviews, trial reviews, and evidence maps.

evidence_categories.rationale: Records separate senolytic mechanism/intervention class from evidence tier, clinical indication, and registry status, reducing the risk that the track becomes an unsupported efficacy bucket.

known_gaps.description: DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies now have PubMed EFetch source snapshots supporting metadata-only abstract-level sample, dosing, timepoint, effect, and safety details, and the legacy IPF generic feasibility/tolerability extraction has been reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. These snapshots do not support full-text/table extraction. Full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements remains incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Retain or ingest full-text/table source snapshots for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements, then reconcile abstract-level extraction against article tables before formal synthesis.

known_gaps.description: Aggregate adverse-event counts, the D+Q bone headache preferred-term row, additional publication-table preferred-term rows, and registry/article discontinuation fields have been extracted for the D+Q bone RCT. IPF and AD-risk now have PubMed EFetch source-snapshot-backed abstract safety extraction, and DKD has a metadata-only source-snapshot-backed record documenting that adverse-event counts were not reported in the retained abstract summary. Full article/table safety extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, D+Q bone supplement material, and harmonized cross-human adverse-event definitions remain incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run retained full-text or table-row extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details, reconcile any D+Q bone supplement safety tables, and create a cross-human safety compatibility group only after event terms, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness are harmonized.

known_gaps.description: Four active no-results registry records are now present, including source-snapshot-backed fisetin frailty registry records for NCT03430037 and NCT03675724. The 2026-06-30 ClinicalTrials.gov surveillance pass also found additional direct active/recruiting senolytic, fisetin, and D+Q candidates, but those records remain deferred because this bounded job retained snapshots only for the two declared fisetin frailty registries.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a follow-up source-snapshot-backed ClinicalTrials.gov pass for additional active/recruiting senolytic hits identified in research/screening-runs/self-healing-coverage-gap-senolytics-registry-surveillance-breadth-followup-3.json, prioritizing NCT02848131, NCT04733534, NCT04785300, NCT05593588, NCT05758246, and NCT06018467; add no-results status records only when retained registry snapshots support them.

known_gaps.description: No recent systematic-review or meta-analysis source has been screened into the knowledge base. This bounded batch did not close the gap because the job supplied no source row or context pack and PubMed/NLM retrieval was unavailable from the worker environment.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a bounded search/screening job for recent systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and trial-landscape reviews; create or refresh PubMed source snapshots before upgrading any review source beyond screened or triage-summary maturity.

next_coverage_action: Run follow-up source-snapshot-backed ClinicalTrials.gov surveillance for additional deferred active/recruiting senolytic hits from the 2026-06-30 screening run, run a dedicated review-landscape search/screening pass, retain full-text or table-row source snapshots for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk before upgrading beyond PubMed abstract extraction, reconcile any D+Q bone supplement safety material, and keep cross-human safety synthesis blocked until event definitions, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness are harmonized.

provenance.note: Original triage-level coverage anchor for the senolytics vertical slice.
Retained full-text adverse-event table lists overall events and preferred-term rows for control and D+Q arms, with blank cells requiring zero/not-reported caution.
Retained full text provides withdrawal/discontinuation details, D+Q dosing cadence over 20 weeks, and durability limitations for short-term versus 20-week bone-turnover effects.
ClinicalTrials.gov retained registry snapshot provides aggregate serious adverse event, other adverse event, and death counts through approximately 20 weeks.
ClinicalTrials.gov retained registry raw payload provides D+Q and untreated-control noncompletion and adverse-event discontinuation counts.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 36857968 abstract FINDINGS and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Metadata-only PubMed EFetch abstract snapshot reports the N=12 randomized IPF pilot design, D 100 mg/day plus Q 1250 mg/day dosing for three consecutive days per week over three weeks, completed dosing/assessment feasibility, non-serious adverse-event totals, no serious adverse events related to D+Q, sleep disturbance/anxiety counts, and safety/feasibility interpretation.

Metadata-only PubMed EFetch abstract snapshot reports D 100 mg plus Q 1250 mg for two days every two weeks over 12 weeks, an N=12 completion sample, no serious adverse events related to the intervention, MoCA change estimates, TNF-alpha percent-change estimate, and TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 31542391 abstract METHODS and FINDINGS
provenance.note: Metadata-only PubMed EFetch abstract snapshot reports the open-label DKD pilot sample (N=9), D 100 mg plus Q 1000 mg for three days, pre/post biosampling 11 days after treatment, and an abstract-level senescent-cell burden signal, but it does not provide adverse-event counts or retained article tables, preserving a safety extraction limitation and blocking full-text/table upgrade.
Retained PMC article markdown identifies a supplementary PDF link, but the supplement tables themselves are not represented as retained normalized table snapshots in this bounded refresh.
Prior bounded registry-surveillance worker preserved the deferred NCT03430037 and NCT03675724 fisetin registry gap because ClinicalTrials.gov source snapshots could not be fetched in that worker.
ClinicalTrials.gov v2 snapshot fetched for follow-up registry surveillance lists NCT03430037 as ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION and contains no resultsSection object.
ClinicalTrials.gov v2 snapshot fetched for follow-up registry surveillance lists NCT03675724 as ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION and contains no resultsSection object.

Bounded review-landscape worker preserved the systematic-review/meta-analysis screening gap because no source row or context pack supplied a concrete candidate and PubMed/NLM retrieval was unavailable in the worker.
